assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'The most common cause is pods that can't be evicted' and lists 'PodDisruptionBudgets preventing pod eviction' as the first 'most likely culprit'

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces' and 'kubectl describe pdb --all-namespaces' commands

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes commands to check pending/terminating pods: 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.phase=Pending' and grep commands for pod status

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Has dedicated section checking node resources with 'kubectl describe nodes | grep -A5 -B5 "Allocated resources"' and 'kubectl top nodes', plus checks for pending pods

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl patch pdb [PDB_NAME] -n [NAMESPACE] --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"minAvailable":0}}'' command to adjust PDBs

assertions.expectation: Mentions that GKE has a PDB timeout (up to 1 hour) after which it may force-drain, and that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB
assertions.reason: No mention of GKE's PDB timeout mechanism or automatic force-drain behavior after 1 hour

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: No mention of surge upgrade settings or configuration adjustments for node pool upgrades

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Webhook issues are not mentioned as a potential cause of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Includes monitoring section with watch commands to track upgrade progress: 'watch -n 30 'kubectl get nodes -o wide'' and operation monitoring

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE disruption-event notifications (POD_PDB_VIOLATION) that alert when eviction is blocked by PDB, available via Cloud Logging or Pub/Sub
assertions.reason: No mention of GKE's disruption-event notifications, POD_PDB_VIOLATION events, or Cloud Logging/Pub/Sub integration
